title: How a protein buries its oily core
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Why does a protein fold at all? Ken Dill's HP model strips the question to its essence: every residue is just H (hydrophobic, oil-like) or P (polar, water-loving), and the chain wanders a square lattice looking for shapes that tuck the H residues away from water. Run the Monte-Carlo search and watch a floppy self-avoiding walk collapse into a compact blob with an oily core and a polar shell — the same hydrophobic effect that drives real proteins into shape. Because the walk is seeded, this fold is perfectly reproducible: seed 42 always finds the same low-energy structure, so anyone can replay and verify the discovery.
